commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Discord skill guidance telling agents to deliver generated images with message-tool attachments or MEDIA lines instead of plain local-path text.

PR surface: Docs +6. Total +6 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. not as a live current-main repro. Source and docs show the relevant generated-media/message-tool delivery path, but the PR provides only a local observation and `git diff --check`, not real after-change proof.

target.title: docs: clarify Discord generated image delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- document how generated images should be delivered through Discord
- call out structured attachments and MEDIA lines so local paths are not mistaken for uploads

## Evidence
- A local Discord delivery test reproduced the failure mode: an image was generated successfully and saved in a local generated-images directory, but the visible reply only contained text, so no uploadable attachment was handed to the Discord gateway.
- Follow-up inspection confirmed the generated image file itself was valid and small enough for Discord; the problem was the delivery handoff, not the image artifact.
- Tool metadata for the OpenClaw-native image generation path already describes the correct completion contract: return generated media as structured attachments or `MEDIA:` paths.
- This docs update records that contract in the Discord skill so future agents do not send local generated-image paths as plain chat text.

## Validation
- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
